summary: Merged a large AI-assisted matching batch: decomp.dev reported 103 new matches, +8856 matched code bytes, and +0.23% code match rate. The PR implemented many former `/// #...` placeholders and `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs across fighter, item, stage, game-mode, library, menu/effect/video, and sysdolphin code, plus typed headers and struct fixes. Review accepted the batch but treated it as a cautionary example: future AI-assisted decomp work should force m2c first, reject raw offset field access, missing prototypes, C99-style declarations, wrong data-section annotations, and unimplemented stub bodies.
what_changed: decomp.dev reported 103 new matches and code match moving to 54.80% (+0.23%, +8856 bytes), with data match +8 bytes.

Implemented many fighter functions, especially Kirby copy/special-N code such as `ftKb_SpecialHi2_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialAirHi1_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialNLoop_IASA`, `ftKb_SpecialAirNLoop_IASA`, `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F9598`, `fn_80105A34`, `ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCC14`, and `ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCCBC`.

Filled common fighter and boss/player functions including `ftCo_800D9C98`, `fn_800DA190`, `ftCo_CaptureLikelike_Anim`, `fn_800D4DD4`, `ftCo_RebirthWait_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Anim`, and `fn_801577B4`.

Implemented many item motion/event functions for Pokemon/items and character articles, including `itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Anim`, `itUnknown_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itHouou_UnkMotion4_Phys`, `itOldottosea_UnkMotion5_Anim`, `itWhitebea_UnkMotion0_Phys`, `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion0_Anim`, and multiple `it_2725_Logic107_*` handlers.

Added stage/game-mode/library/menu matches such as `grCastle_801CDFD8`, `grPushOn_80218330`, `grRCruise_80201918`, `gm_801883C0`, `gm_801BEBF8`, `lbMthp_8001F800`, `fn_800259A0`, and `mnGallery_80258D50`.

Updated prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to typed signatures in several headers, for example `fn_800DA190(Fighter_GObj*)`, `gm_801883C0(void)`, `gm_801BEBF8(u8)`, `it_802B3EFC(Item_GObj*)`, and `mnGallery_80258D50(void*)`.

Adjusted types and structs to support matches, including `itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars.xDD8` becoming `Item_GObj*`, `itFlipper_ItemVars.xDEC` becoming `HSD_JObj*`, new fields in `itHououAttr` and `itUnknownAttributes`, and `HitCapsuleState` gaining `HitCapsule_Unk4`.

Review-related cleanup is visible in the final diff: `ftCo_800D9C98` uses named Fighter callback fields (`death2_cb`, `take_dmg_2_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`) instead of the raw offsets shown in review, and `ftCo_DownBound` uses `fp->mv.co.downspot.x4` instead of `M2C_FIELD(fp, ..., 0x2344)`.
smart_moves: The PR tied the large batch to a measurable decomp.dev result rather than only claiming local success: +0.23% matched code and 103 new matches.
The author described a verifier workflow and explicitly intended to filter regressions and LLM cheats; review then identified concrete extra rejection rules to add.
Several final changes prefer project-level typed fields over raw offsets, notably `ftCo_800D9C98` and `ftCo_DownBound`.
Header prototype updates made many new matches callable and type-checked instead of leaving `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders.
Some noisy includes and ad hoc declarations were reduced, for example in `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`, which moved toward smaller include surfaces and static local declarations.

The author accepted the review lesson and said future agent runs would force m2c first and reject raw pointer arithmetic, `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs, and C99 loop-variable declarations before accepting writes.
decomp_lessons: AI-assisted matching is acceptable in this repo only if the output matches and does not introduce weird or low-quality C; maintainers explicitly said AI usage itself was not the problem.
Always run m2c as a first pass before asking an LLM to write C; reviewers expected m2c-style field access or proper struct fields instead of blind byte offsets.
Prefer real struct fields. If fields are not yet known, `M2C_FIELD` is preferred over raw `*(s32*)((u8*)fp + offset)` casts, but the best outcome is to create or use the proper field.
Do not add a function body containing only `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`; leave the `/// #symbol` marker for unmatched code unless the function is genuinely matched.
Data section placement matters: reviewers corrected attempts to mark `ViCharaDesc` globals as `SDATA`; they were `.sbss`, not `.sdata`.
The project is constrained by C89/MWCC style: variables should be declared at the top of scope, and C99 loop-variable declarations can fail checks.
Use the Issues/decomp.dev report as a gate, not just a dashboard: it surfaced missing prototypes, C99 issues, field-access problems, and reported unmatched-item regressions.
Large automated batches should be split or heavily filtered because reviewers may accept a net-positive batch while still leaving cleanup debt.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept project address names such as `fn_800DA190`, `fn_80105A34`, `gm_801883C0`, and `it_802E3098`.
Stage functions follow module prefixes plus addresses, e.g. `grCastle_801CDFD8`, `grPushOn_80218330`, `grRCruise_80201918`, and `grIceMt_801F8398`.
Item motion/event functions use existing item-state naming such as `itOldottosea_UnkMotion5_Anim`, `itWhitebea_UnkMotion0_Phys`, and `it_2725_Logic107_DmgDealt`.
Header prototypes use address comments like `/* 1883C0 */ f32 gm_801883C0(void);` and static forward declarations such as `/* 105A34 */ static void fn_80105A34(Fighter_GObj* gobj);`.
Common pointer local names in the diff follow existing shorthand: `fp` for `Fighter*`, `ip` for `Item*`, `gp` for `Ground*`, and `gobj` for `HSD_GObj`/typed GObj pointers.
Established callback field names such as `death2_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`, `pre_hitlag_cb`, `post_hitlag_cb`, and `accessory4_cb` should be used instead of raw field offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The batch frequently used MWCC-matching shape helpers such as `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, dummy `unused` locals, manual temporaries, and gotos to influence stack layout and control flow.
Several matches used explicit byte/offset arithmetic for unknown structures; reviewers treated this as slop and recommended `M2C_FIELD` or proper fields.
Some functions use match-oriented gotos and labels such as `return_error`, `ret_true`, and `check_upper` to preserve compiler output.

The diff contains guarded `MWERKS_GEKKO` inline `rlwimi` bit insertion in places like `grCastle_801CDFD8` and `it_802AC58C`; this is evidence of matching pressure, but future AI output should avoid assembly cheats where possible.
Typed header fixes and local static declarations were used as matching enablers when formerly unknown external functions became callable.
review_feedback: PsiLupan advised checking the Issues run for missing function prototypes and C99 rule violations such as variables declared inside loops or non-top-level scopes.
PsiLupan rejected `SDATA static ViCharaDesc` in `vi0401.c`: the symbol was `.sbss`, not `.sdata`.
PsiLupan made the same `.sbss` versus `.sdata` correction for `vi0501.c`.

ribbanya called raw Fighter pointer arithmetic in `ftCo_800D9C98` slop and said `M2C_FIELD` would be preferred if proper fields were unavailable; ideally the existing `Fighter` callback fields should be used.
ribbanya suggested reverting a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body in `grPushOn_802183E4` back to the placeholder marker `/// #grPushOn_802183E4`.

ribbanya initially requested changes because of frequent field-access problems and regressions, then later accepted the functions as-is while recommending not continuing this workflow until m2c was integrated as a first pass.
ribbanya said AI usage is not a problem as long as it matches and does not do anything too weird.
dwilliams27 responded that future verifier logic would reject raw offset pointer arithmetic, `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs, and C99 loop-variable declarations.
